items.title: Dependencies + impeccable skills installed
items.expected: three + @types/three + gsap in frontend/package.json; impeccable skills present; PRODUCT.md + DESIGN.md at root
items.evidence: three@^0.185, gsap@^3.15, @types/three@^0.185 installed; .claude/skills/impeccable written; PRODUCT.md + DESIGN.md created

items.title: TypeScript + lint clean
items.expected: tsc exits 0; lint exits with 0 errors
items.evidence: tsc=0; lint 0 errors (4 advisory warnings: 2 react-refresh on theme.tsx, 1 function-length Dashboard, 1 ImportDialog)

items.title: Three.js + GSAP code-split into async chunk
items.expected: ThreeBackdrop chunk is separate from the main index bundle (first paint not blocked)
items.evidence: dist/assets/ThreeBackdrop-*.js = 156 KB gzip, separate from index-*.js = 160 KB gzip

items.title: Original impeccable detect findings fixed
items.expected: side-stripe border (request-echo) and width-transition (batch-pip) findings are gone
items.evidence: Both original findings resolved. New 'em-dash-overuse (522)' finding scans CSS code comments, not UI copy — waived.
items.waiver: em-dash-overuse counts em-dashes in index.css code comments (not rendered body copy); non-actionable false positive.

items.title: Light + dark render correctly on every screen
steps: 2. Toggle theme via the bottom-left control
3. Visit projects, project detail, the new-session wizard, a ticket, and open the import/add-bit modals
items.expected: Every screen and modal reads correctly in both light and dark; no unstyled/contrast-broken surfaces; accent stays the single warm highlight

items.title: Theme toggle persists across reload
steps: 1. Set light theme via the toggle
items.expected: Light theme is still applied after reload (no flash of dark first)

items.title: WCAG AA contrast holds in both themes
steps: 1. Spot-check body text, muted text, and placeholders on canvas + surface in each theme
items.expected: Body ≥ 4.5:1, large/bold ≥ 3:1, placeholders ≥ 4.5:1, in light and dark

items.title: WebGL backdrop (removed)
items.expected: N/A — the animated backdrop was removed at user request (Rev 3). Depth is a static CSS vignette; Three.js/GSAP uninstalled.
items.evidence: Feature removed; no motion behind content.
items.notes: Marked N/A — no longer in scope.

items.title: Acceptable FPS on a mid-tier device
steps: 1. Observe scrolling/interaction smoothness with the backdrop active
items.expected: No perceptible jank; interaction stays responsive
